question_text: A student performs an experiment with a wooden block on a horizontal table. She measures that a horizontal force of 5 N causes the block to move at a constant velocity. Which statement best explains this observation?

options.label: A
options.text: The applied force of 5 N is greater than the friction force, so the block accelerates.

options.label: B
options.text: The friction force equals 5 N, so the horizontal forces are balanced and the block moves at constant velocity.

options.label: C
options.text: There is no friction force acting on the block because it is moving at constant velocity.

options.label: D
options.text: The applied force is doing work against gravity, which balances the friction force.

explanation: When an object moves at constant velocity, its speed and direction are not changing, which means the forces must be balanced. The 5 N applied force must be balanced by an equal friction force of 5 N acting in the opposite direction. If friction were smaller, the block would accelerate.
